title: What is Teacher Forcing?
concept: Teacher forcing trains sequential models by feeding the ground-truth target word from the dataset to the next decoder step, rather than the model's own (potentially incorrect) prediction.

exercises.prompt: How does a text-generating AI typically write a long sentence?

options.text: It uses its previous words to help predict the next word.
It predicts all words at the exact same millisecond.
It ignores everything it wrote before.

exercises.explain: In sequence models like text generators, the word generated at step 1 is typically used as the input to help predict the word at step 2.

cards.title: Keeping AI on Track
cards.body: When AI models learn to generate text, they predict word by word. But if they make a tiny mistake on the very first word, the entire sentence can quickly turn into complete gibberish. How do we keep them from getting lost in their own mistakes?

cards.title: What is Teacher Forcing?
cards.body: Teacher Forcing is a training technique for sequence-based models (like translation AIs). Instead of feeding the model's own (often incorrect) output into the next step, we feed it the actual correct word (ground-truth) from our dataset. This keeps the model on the right track!

cards.title: The Piano Teacher
cards.body: Imagine learning to play a song on the piano. Without Teacher Forcing, if you hit a wrong note, you might get confused and play the rest of the song completely wrong. With Teacher Forcing, a teacher gently nudges your hand back to the correct key after every single note, ensuring you practice the rest of the song from the right starting points!

cards.title: An English-to-French Example
cards.body:
Say our AI is learning to translate "Hi" to French ("Salut").
Step 1: The AI incorrectly guesses "Sausage".
With Teacher Forcing: We ignore the error and feed "Salut" into Step 2 anyway.
Without Teacher Forcing: We feed "Sausage" into Step 2, and the AI gets completely lost trying to translate the rest.

cards.title: Only for Practice!
cards.body: You might think Teacher Forcing is used when the model is actually generating answers for users. But it's not! Teacher Forcing is only used during training. When the model is deployed and chatting with real people, it has to rely on its own predictions without a teacher's help.

cards.title: Training Paths Compared
cards.body: See how training behaves when using Teacher Forcing compared to letting the model feed on its own outputs.

left.text: Feed the correct targets at each step. Errors don't stack up, keeping the model on track.

right.text: Feed the model's own guesses. One early mistake can spiral into absolute nonsense.

exercises.prompt: What is a major problem when training a model WITHOUT Teacher Forcing?

options.text: The model instantly corrects itself without any help.
A single early mistake can ruin the entire rest of the sentence.
The model refuses to generate any more words.

exercises.explain: Without teacher forcing, a single mistake early on causes the model to base all future predictions on that error, leading to a cascade of bad outputs.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Teacher forcing is used when a model is deployed to real users to help it correct its mistakes in real-time.
exercises.explain: Teacher forcing is only used during the training phase to speed up learning. During actual testing or deployment, the model must rely on its own previous outputs.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to describe what we feed the model during training.
exercises.sentence: Teacher forcing feeds the _____ target word into the next step of the model.
exercises.explain: During training with teacher forcing, the model receives the correct ground-truth word at each step instead of its own predicted output.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI outputs looks like it suffered from a runaway error loop because it did NOT use Teacher Forcing during training?

options.text: AI: "The weather is lovely today, isn't it?"
AI: "The weather is is is is is."
AI: "It is raining outside, grab an umbrella."

exercises.explain: Option A is created by an AI helper utilizing Teacher Forcing (correcting the sequence step-by-step). Option B shows an AI that got lost in its own mistakes, repeating gibberish.

exercises.prompt: Why does Teacher Forcing help sequential AI models learn much faster during the early stages of training?

options.text: It feeds actual target tokens at each step, preventing early mistakes from ruining the rest of the training sequence.
It forces the model to work twice as hard by hiding the correct answers during training.
It relies entirely on the model's own predictions to build independence from day one.
It completely deletes the decoder part of the model to simplify calculations.

exercises.explain: Teacher forcing feeds correct ground-truth targets at each training step to prevent errors from snowballing, leading to much faster convergence during the early phases of training.
